message:
✨ Port github-pipelines to Go backend for localhost/in-cluster deploys (#8408)

* ✨ Port github-pipelines to Go backend for localhost/in-cluster deploys

The Netlify Function at web/netlify/functions/github-pipelines.mts only
serves console.kubestellar.io. Without a matching Go handler, users who
install the console on localhost or in a cluster get demo data on the
/ci-cd pipeline cards instead of live GitHub Actions data.

This mirrors the existing nightly_e2e.go pattern (which has a parallel
Go + Netlify implementation of the same endpoint for the same reason).

### New: pkg/api/handlers/github_pipelines.go

Same six views as the Netlify function, same JSON shapes — the TS hook
(`web/src/hooks/useGitHubPipelines.ts`) works against either backend
without branching:

  - GET  ?view=pulse          — latest Release run + streak + 14-day strip
  - GET  ?view=matrix&days=N  — per-workflow × daily heatmap (14/30/90)
  - GET  ?view=flow           — in-progress / queued runs with jobs+steps
  - GET  ?view=failures       — last 10 failed runs with failing step
  - GET  ?view=log            — log tail of a failed job
  - POST ?view=mutate&op=…    — rerun / cancel (disabled by default)

### Caching / history

- In-memory response cache, 2-min TTL, per-(view,repo,days,job) key.
- singleflight.Group coalesces concurrent cold fetches (same trick as
  nightly_e2e for #7053).
- Rolling 90-day history blob (sync.RWMutex over nested map). Lost on
  restart; re-seeded from GitHub on next matrix fetch. Since GitHub's
  own retention is 14 days, restart only thins the 30/90-day views
  until history accumulates again.

### Security

- Repo allowlist (same 6 repos as /hygiene): console, docs, console-kb,
  kubestellar-mcp, console-marketplace, homebrew-tap. Unknown repos
  rejected with 400 — prevents arbitrary GitHub API calls via the
  user-controlled `repo` query param, matching the isAllowedRepo
  pattern in nightly_e2e.
- Mutations gated on GITHUB_MUTATIONS_TOKEN env var. If unset — the
  default on every deployment — POST returns 503. Operator opts in by
  setting a PAT with `actions:write` scope.
- Log fetches include the owner/repo in the URL path but are also
  gated on the allowlist.
- Error bodies read through LimitReader (10KB cap) to prevent unbounded
  memory on large error pages, same pattern as nightly_e2e.

### Wiring

Registered in pkg/api/server.go under the authed `/api` group next to
the existing nightly-e2e handler. Both GET and POST on the same path —
the handler dispatches on `?view=` and method.

### Tests

10 new tests in github_pipelines_test.go:
  - missing GITHUB_TOKEN → 500
  - unknown view → 400
  - mutations disabled without GITHUB_MUTATIONS_TOKEN → 503
  - mutation repo allowlist
  - mutation op allowlist
  - GET on mutate view → 405
  - log requires repo + job params
  - ghpHistory merge (newest run per day wins) + retention trim
  - ghpIsAllowedRepo
  - ghpStreakKind (success / failure / timed_out / cancelled / nil)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix pkg/k8s fake dynamic client panic (pre-existing on main)

ConsoleWatcher and ConsoleResources tests used
NewSimpleDynamicClient which panics on List() calls because the
console CRD list kinds (ManagedWorkloadList, ClusterGroupList,
WorkloadDeploymentList) aren't registered.

Fix: use NewSimpleDynamicClientWithCustomListKinds with a shared
consoleGVRListKinds map. This matches the pattern already used in
argocd_test.go and lazy_client_hot_reload_test.go in the same
package.

Pre-existing breakage on main — same class of issue that blocked
the nightly for 4 days (issues #7256, #7898, #8091, #8313). The
new go-test.yml gate from PR #8354 correctly catches it on PRs now.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix pulse: include workflow_dispatch runs, not just schedule

The pulse view filtered `event=schedule`, which excluded manually-
triggered nightly runs (`workflow_dispatch`). After today's manual
trigger of v0.3.21-nightly.20260416, the pulse was still showing
v0.3.20-nightly.20260408 with 4 failures — even though the manual
run succeeded. Dropping the event filter fixes this.

The Release workflow only triggers on `schedule` and
`workflow_dispatch`, so removing the filter doesn't introduce
noise from push/PR events.

Fixed in both the Netlify Function and the Go handler.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -7,9 +7,12 @@ import (
 	"github.com/kubestellar/console/pkg/api/v1alpha1"
 	metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
-	"k8s.io/client-go/dynamic/fake"
+	fake "k8s.io/client-go/dynamic/fake"
 )
 
+// consoleGVRListKinds is defined in watcher_lifecycle_test.go (same package).
+// We reuse it here to avoid duplication.
+
 func TestConsoleResources(t *testing.T) {
 	scheme := runtime.NewScheme()
 
@@ -41,7 +44,7 @@ func TestConsoleResources(t *testing.T) {
 	}
 	wdU, _ := wd.ToUnstructured()
 
-	fakeDyn := fake.NewSimpleDynamicClient(scheme, mwU, cgU, wdU)
+	fakeDyn := fake.NewSimpleDynamicClientWithCustomListKinds(scheme, consoleGVRListKinds, mwU, cgU, wdU)
 	cp := NewConsolePersistence(fakeDyn)
 
 	ctx := context.Background()
